_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice based on 2015 levels set at 100, currently stands at 132. This reflects an overall increase of about 32% since 2015, indicating a notable rise in these commodities' prices. The increase has been uneven over the period, with certain items experiencing sharper climbs than others. This recent price climb highlights shifting dynamics in global soft commodity markets.
notes.movers: Among the basket components, coffee prices have surged the most, rising 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 22%, wheat at 7%, and sugar at 5%. These changes are driven primarily by weather-related events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought affecting coffee growing regions, and issues like citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice production. Rather than reflecting broad inflationary trends, these price movements illustrate the volatility typical of thinly traded soft commodity markets sensitive to supply disruptions.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is largely attributable to a few weather-exposed crops undergoing significant stress. Given the nature of these agricultural inputs, prices may remain elevated for several years, reflecting ongoing vulnerability to environmental factors rather than general economic inflation.
